name: La Alpujarra – circular between Pampaneira and Bubión via the Poqueira river
name_original: La Alpujarra – circular entre Pampaneira y Bubión por el río Poqueira
region_intro: Hiking around Pampaneira offers access to the Sierra Nevada mountains and the dramatic Barranco de Poqueira (Poqueira Gorge). The region features diverse landscapes, including lush river valleys, holm oak and gall oak forests, and views of high peaks like Mulhacén. Trails often connect the white villages nestled within the gorge, providing varied terrain for exploration. Best hiking trails around Pampaneira The most popular hiking route is La Alpujarra – circular between Pampaneira and Bubión via the Poqueira river, a 4.3 miles (6.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 33 minutes to complete. This moderate route connects two of the famous white villages along the Poqueira River. Another top favourite among local hikers is Poqueira River – Capileira Village loop from Pampaneira, a moderate 5.3 miles (8.6 km) path. This trail leads through the heart of the Poqueira Gorge, offering views of the valley and connecting to Capileira. Local hikers also love the Fuente Agria Ferruginosa – Poqueira River Waterfall loop from Pampaneira, a 1.9 miles (3.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain to a waterfall, often completed in about 54 minutes. Hiking around Pampaneira is defined by the deep Poqueira Gorge, views of the Sierra Nevada peaks, and diverse forests of holm and gall oaks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Pampaneira are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 1,600 reviews. More than 7,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Pampaneira's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of hiking trails can I expect around Pampaneira?
A: Pampaneira, situated in the dramatic Barranco de Poqueira (Poqueira Gorge) within the Sierra Nevada National and Natural Park, offers a diverse range of hiking trails. You'll find routes winding through lush river valleys, dense holm and gall oak forests, and paths connecting the picturesque white villages of the Alpujarra. Many trails also provide spectacular views of the high peaks like Mulhacén and Veleta.

Q: Are there any easy hiking routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed stroll?
A: Yes, there are several easy options. For a shorter, accessible walk, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10304910" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Agria Ferruginosa – Poqueira River Waterfall loop from Pampaneira&lt;/a&gt;. This 3.1 km (1.9 miles) trail takes about 54 minutes and leads through varied terrain to a waterfall, perfect for a gentle introduction to the area's natural beauty.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes starting from Pampaneira?
A: Absolutely. Many popular routes in the area are circular, allowing you to return to your starting point without retracing your steps. A great moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980664491/la-alpujarra-circular-between-pampaneira-and-bubion-via-the-poqueira-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Alpujarra – circular between Pampaneira and Bubión via the Poqueira river&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.9 km (4.3 miles) long and takes around 2 hours 33 minutes, connecting two of the famous white villages.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: For those seeking a more demanding experience, Pampaneira offers difficult trails with significant elevation changes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14601111" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Agria Ferruginosa – View of Pampaneira loop from Pampaneira&lt;/a&gt;. This route spans 16.5 km (10.3 miles) and involves substantial ascents and descents, typically requiring over 6 hours to complete.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see while hiking?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and cultural landmarks. You'll hike through the dramatic Poqueira Gorge, with views of the towering Sierra Nevada peaks like Mulhacén and Veleta. Along the trails, you might encounter the traditional white villages of Pampaneira, Bubión, and Capileira. For specific viewpoints, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/310985" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajos del Ángel Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/154326" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Veleta viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; for stunning panoramas.

Q: Are there any routes that connect the white villages of the Poqueira Gorge?
A: Yes, connecting the white villages is a highlight of hiking in this area. The well-known 'Ruta de los Tres Pueblos' is a popular option. A specific route you can take is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38273252" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poqueira River – Capileira Village loop from Pampaneira&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 8.6 km (5.3 miles) path that leads through the heart of the gorge and connects to Capileira.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails around Pampaneira?
A: The hiking trails around Pampaneira are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 1,600 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning natural scenery, the charm of the white villages, and the well-maintained paths that cater to various skill levels.

Q: Is Pampaneira a good destination for family-friendly hikes?
A: Pampaneira offers several trails suitable for families, particularly the shorter and easier routes. The diverse landscapes, including lush valleys and forests, provide an engaging environment for children. Look for trails with minimal elevation gain and shorter distances for a comfortable family outing.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Pampaneira?
A: Generally, the trails in the Sierra Nevada National and Natural Park, including those around Pampaneira, are dog-friendly. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in protected areas, to respect wildlife and other hikers. Ensure you carry enough water for both yourself and your pet.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Pampaneira?
A: The best time to hike in Pampaneira is typically during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, and the landscapes are particularly vibrant with spring blooms or autumn colors. Summer can be very hot, especially at lower altitudes, while winter may bring snow to higher elevations, making some trails more challenging.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see on the hikes?
A: Yes, you can find waterfalls in the area. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10304910" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Agria Ferruginosa – Poqueira River Waterfall loop from Pampaneira&lt;/a&gt; is an easy trail specifically designed to lead you to a waterfall, offering a refreshing natural sight.

Q: What kind of forests can I expect to hike through?
A: The hillsides around Pampaneira feature natural forests abundant with holm oaks and gall oaks, particularly in shadier sections. These diverse forests provide pleasant, shaded paths and contribute to the rich biodiversity of the region, offering a different kind of scenery compared to the open gorge views.
tips: Pampaneira, one of the most beautiful towns in the Alpujarra of Granada and totally recommended to visit.
This waterfall is a good example of the force with which the waters of the Poqueira River fall, traditionally used for irrigation and today for power generation.

This beautiful bridge helps you cross the waters of the Poqueira River and is located next to the ruins of an old flour mill. There is a plaque with very interesting information about the operation of the mills.
Originated by the confluence of multiple streams from Mulhacén and Veleta

El Barranco del Poqueira is one of the most visited natural spaces in the province of Granada. This impressive site on the southern slope of the Sierra Nevada slopes steeply from the summit of Veleta. It includes villages of great beauty on its mountain slopes: Bubión is one of them. The village was put forward by the Junta de Andalucía (Andalusian government). This is how it has become over the years, a choice of prime importance for tourism in the region.

waypoints.name: Bridge by the ruins of the old water mill on the Poqueira River
